title: auth — PHP attribute contracts
description: Two attributes. #[AsAuthListener] marks a class as a subscriber to auth-lifecycle events (login / logout / refresh / reuse-detection / cross-app-grant / impersonation / JWT rotation). #[RegistrationPolicy] marks a class as a domain-specific registration policy — discovered at boot, resolved per RegistrationContext type. Both are discovered by the AuthServiceProvider at boot so downstream services react without explicit event bindings.

attributes.name: AsAuthListener

params.name: events
params.description: Which auth events this class reacts to. Wildcard `*` = every event under `Stackra\Auth\Events\*`. Specific list e.g. `['UserLoggedIn', 'RefreshTokenReuseDetected']` subscribes to those only. Validated against the events map at boot; typos abort application boot.

params.name: queue
params.description: Queue name for asynchronous dispatch. `null` = synchronous (reserved for observability tap-outs). Common values: `notifications` (default), `notifications-critical` (P0 events — refresh-token reuse, JWT deny-list adds, impersonation start), `audit` (write-heavy compliance streams).

params.name: priority
params.description: Listener execution order. Lower runs first. Use for cases where one listener's side-effect must precede another (e.g. `RevokeAllActiveSessionsListener` before `SendPasswordChangedFromNewDeviceNotification`). Framework default listeners occupy 1–99; user-defined listeners default to 100.

attributes.name: RegistrationPolicy

params.name: contextType
params.description: Fully-qualified class name of the RegistrationContext subclass this policy handles. E.g. `Stackra\Sports\Athletes\Data\AthleteRegistrationContext`. The default `GenericRegistrationPolicy` handles `Stackra\Auth\Data\GenericRegistrationContext`. Discovery matches a context to its policy via this attribute at boot.

params.name: priority
params.description: When multiple policies match a context (rare — domain module overrides the default), the LOWEST priority wins. Framework's `GenericRegistrationPolicy` runs at priority 1000; domain-specific policies default to 100 so they naturally override.
